task.title: Permanently reusing the same IP address on different interfaces

li.p: You are logged in as the root user.
The network interfaces are not configured.

context.p: You can use the virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) feature to permanently use the same IP address on different interfaces in one server.

note.p: To enable remote peers to contact both VRF interfaces while reusing the same IP address, the network interfaces must belong to different broadcasting domains. A broadcast domain in a network is a set of nodes, which receive broadcast traffic sent by any of them. In most configurations, all nodes connected to the same switch belong to the same broadcasting domain.

step.cmd: Create and configure the first VRF device:

li.p: Create a connection for the VRF device and assign it to a routing table. For example, to create a VRF device named vrf0 that is assigned to the 1001 routing table:
li.codeblock: # nmcli connection add type vrf ifname vrf0 con-name vrf0 table 1001 ipv4.method disabled ipv6.method disabled

li.p: Assign a network device to the VRF just created. For example, to add the enp1s0 Ethernet device to the vrf0 VRF device and assign an IP address and the subnet mask to enp1s0, enter:
li.codeblock: # nmcli connection add type ethernet con-name vrf.enp1s0 ifname enp1s0 controller vrf0 ipv4.method manual ipv4.address 192.0.2.1/24

step.cmd: Create and configure the next VRF device:

li.p: Create the VRF device and assign it to a routing table. For example, to create a VRF device named vrf1 that is assigned to the 1002 routing table, enter:
li.codeblock: # nmcli connection add type vrf ifname vrf1 con-name vrf1 table 1002 ipv4.method disabled ipv6.method disabled

li.p: Assign a network device to the VRF just created. For example, to add the enp7s0 Ethernet device to the vrf1 VRF device and assign an IP address and the subnet mask to enp7s0, enter:
li.codeblock: # nmcli connection add type ethernet con-name vrf.enp7s0 ifname enp7s0 controller vrf1 ipv4.method manual ipv4.address 192.0.2.1/24
